title: From the Hip | Casual Photos of Contradictions & Conundrums
description: We live in a world where inconsistency, paradox and irony flourishes while commodities attempt to trump community. We’re inundated by photographs of products and services for sale, many are clever, but lack soul. Meanwhile, almost everyone has a camera on their phone and photography is immensely popular. It can be rewarding – there are many campfires tended to by the positive and playful. Expressions of this impulse are portrayed in photographs that provide another point of view, a smile, a good laugh or even an insight., Photographer and experimental geographer, Hank Meals, has stalked and stumbled into off-handed imagery for years and he’s assembled a collection in his latest slide show From the Hip: Casual Photos of Contradictions and Conundrums., Hank will provide ongoing commentary informed by his background in anthropology, history, geography, art and a simple version of the good life. Here’s an opportunity to expand your views while increasing your pleasure and perception with this entertaining and informative presentation.

title: Lucinda Williams
description: Lucinda Williams brings her band to the Marisa Funk Theater at The Center for the Arts in Grass Valley on June 12. The New York Times Magazine just named her one of the 30 greatest American singer-songwriters — and she's arriving with a brand new album to prove it. World's Gone Wrong, her eighteenth studio album, was written and recorded in a matter of weeks this spring out of pure urgency — she set aside a nearly finished record to make it. Forty-five years in, three Grammys, and Car Wheels on a Gravel Road in her catalog, Williams is still making music that feels like it had to exist. This is not a nostalgia tour. This is an artist with something to say.

title: But I'm a Cheerleader
description: 6/14 - The Onyx Downtown is incredibly proud to once again partner with Nevada County Pride for this special, one-night-only screening at the Nevada Theatre of the cult classic BUT I’M A CHEERLEADER! In this classic satire directed by Jamie Babbit, parents who are worried that their children might not be walking the straight and narrow path discover a rehabilitation camp designed to curb alternative lifestyles. Megan (Natasha Lyonne), a high school student and member of the cheerleading squad, seems like an ordinary enough teenage girl, but her habit of honestly expressing herself and lack of romantic enthusiasm for her boyfriend convince her very repressed parents that Megan is becoming a lesbian. So Megan is shipped off to True Directions, a camp for gay and gay-leaning teens, where Mary Brown (Cathy Moriarty) and Mike (RuPaul) attempt to deprogram kids with homosexual tendencies.

title: Ripple Effect: Art + Music + Psychedelia w/ Chamber Project
venue_name: Center for the Arts | Granucci Gallery
description: Ripple Effect: ART + MUSIC + PSYCHEDELIA, Opening June 19, 2026, The Center for the Arts, Grass Valley, The Center for the Arts’ Gallery Curator, Sarah Coleman, and local gallery owner Brian Chambers have joined forces to present Ripple Effect: ART + MUSIC + PSYCHEDELIA, an immersive exhibition exploring the visual and cultural legacy of the psychedelic era., Featuring a curated selection of paintings, drawings, and photographs—both historic and contemporary—the exhibition recreates the moment when psychedelic experiences began reshaping music, art, and global culture. Iconic imagery, including photographs of Bob Dylan and Joni Mitchell, is presented alongside original Grateful Dead album artwork and rare vintage concert posters. These works are interwoven with contemporary interpretations, offering fresh, vibrant expressions inspired by the pioneers of psychedelic art. Think Jen Stark., At the heart of the exhibition is The Chambers Project, widely regarded as a leading force in contemporary psychedelic art. Founded by Brian Chambers, the gallery has become an international hub for artists, collectors, and enthusiasts. With longstanding relationships with influential figures such as Ralph Steadman, Roger Dean, the Rick Griffin Estate, Oliver Vernon, and Mars-1, The Chambers Project continues to shape and elevate the genre on a global scale., More than a gallery, The Chambers Project fosters a dynamic cultural community. Through events, workshops, and discussions, it creates space for meaningful dialogue around the intersections of art, psychedelia, and consciousness—bridging generations of artists and audiences alike., Ripple Effect invites viewers into a multi-sensory journey—one that reflects the enduring influence of a transformative era while showcasing the evolving language of psychedelic art today.

title: Do the Right Thing
description: 6/21 - Set on one block of Brooklyn’s Bed-Stuy Do or Die neighborhood at the height of summer, this 1989 masterpiece by Spike Lee confirmed him as a writer and filmmaker of peerless vision and passionate social engagement. Over the course of a single day, the easygoing interactions of a cast of unforgettable characters—Da Mayor, Mother Sister, Mister Señor Love Daddy, Tina, Sweet Dick Willie, Buggin Out, Radio Raheem, Sal, Pino, Vito, and Lee’s Mookie among them—give way to heated confrontations as tensions rise along racial fault lines, ultimately exploding into violence. Punctuated by the anthemic refrain of Public Enemy’s “Fight the Power,” DO THE RIGHT THING is a landmark in American cinema, as politically and emotionally charged and as relevant now as when it first hit the big screen.
